attributes.best_description:
-The session is for Figure Skaters FS1 or higher.
-Priority of right of way is in the following order:
          1) Skaters in Programs
          2) Skaters in Lesson
          3) All other freestyle skaters
-Please be mindful of your surroundings


Freestyle Rules:

- All Skaters must check in at Front desk before entering Ice
- NO Virtual/Zoom Lessons
- NO Refunds, Credits, Session changes or exchanges.
- NO Waitlist for Full Sessions

IceSymmetrics - Full Series
IceSymmetrics is composed of skaters who skated as kids, most of them competitively and generally have some previous synchro experience (though not all do). Many of the skaters, at some point, passed Junior or Senior Moves in the Field (Pre-Gold or Gold Skating Skills) and/or have passed some Silver Dances.

-The session is for Figure Skaters FS1 or higher.
-Priority of right of way is in the following order:
          1) Skaters in Programs
          2) Skaters in Lesson
          3) All other freestyle skaters
-Please be mindful of your surroundings


Freestyle Rules:

- All Skaters must check in at Front desk before entering Ice
- NO Virtual/Zoom Lessons
- NO Refunds, Credits, Session changes or exchanges.
- NO Waitlist for Full Sessions

Cold Fusion - Full Series
Cold Fusion is composed of skaters who mostly began skating as adults and didn’t have any synchro experience when they began with the team. Many of the skaters have passed the Adult Pre-Bronze or Bronze Skating Skills (Moves in the Field) test (Edges, 3-turns, mohawks, strong crossovers forward & backward).

-The session is for Figure Skaters FS1 or higher.
-Priority of right of way is in the following order:
          1) Skaters in Programs
          2) Skaters in Lesson
          3) All other freestyle skaters
-Please be mindful of your surroundings


Freestyle Rules:

- All Skaters must check in at Front desk before entering Ice
- NO Virtual/Zoom Lessons
- NO Refunds, Credits, Session changes or exchanges.
- NO Waitlist for Full Sessions

All skaters that need skating aids (e.g. buckets or seals) should register for the Tiny Tot series before beginning Hockey 1. Youth Hockey 1 - 2 - 3 These classes are open to children ages 6 and up and is an introductory class to the fundamentals of hockey skating. A skater should have already completed a skating class: Tiny Tots, PreHockey, Youth Beginner 1, or another comparable class at another rink to join the HOCKEY 1 class. Full gear is required for these classes.

Note: Class time goes from 8:45-9:30am. Hockey 2 & 3 enrollment is contingent on completing the previous level. FOR HOCKEY 2/3: A proper hockey helmet, with a cage is required and full gear is required.
Note: Class time goes from 8:45am-9:30am. FOR HOCKEY 2/3: A proper hockey helmet, with a cage is required and full gear is required.

NOTE: No sticks or pucks are allowed on the ice during ice skating lessons. Parent & Tot is a one hour skating session designed especially for our youngest tots. A 15-minute lesson will be offered each week and the rink will be set up with games and activities for you to play with your tot. The session is for 2–5-year-old skaters accompanied by one parent. Cost includes skate rental and admission for parent and tot. There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

PLEASE DO NOT ENROLL IN A CLASS NOT APPROPRIATE TO YOUR LEVEL!The adult curriculum is designed for beginning, intermediate and experienced adult skaters who wish to improve their skating skills. Participation in the program will help promote physical fitness and improve balance and coordination while teaching proper skating techniques. Group instruction includes a 30 minute class and 30 minute supervised practice, Beginner 1 (Pre-Alpha equivalent) & Adult Beginner 1 1. Two foot glide 2. One-foot glide/ right foot/ left foot 3. Forward Swizzle 4. Backward Wiggle 5. Backward Swizzle 6. Forward Pumps

sPLEASE DO NOT ENROLL IN A CLASS NOT APPROPRIATE TO YOUR LEVEL!The adult curriculum is designed for beginning, intermediate and experienced adult skaters who wish to improve their skating skills. Participation in the program will help promote physical fitness and improve balance and coordination while teaching proper skating techniques. Group instruction includes a 30 minute class and 30 minute supervised practice, Beginner 2 (Alpha equivalent) & Adult Beginner 2 1. Forward Stroking 2. Forward Crossovers left over right (5) 3. Forward Crossovers right over left (5) 4. One foot snowplow stop 5. Two foot turn   Beginner 3 (Beta equivalent) & Adult Beginner 3 1. Backward Crossovers right over left (5) 2. Backward Crossovers left over right (5) 3. Backward Stroking (6) 4. Right T-Stop 5. Left T-Stop 6. Introduction to Forward Edges   I

PLEASE DO NOT ENROLL IN A CLASS NOT APPROPRIATE TO YOUR LEVEL!The adult curriculum is designed for beginning, intermediate and experienced adult skaters who wish to improve their skating skills. Intermediate 1 (Gamma equivalent) & Adult Intermediate 1. Left Forward Outside 3-Turn 2. Right Forward Outside 3-Turn 3. Left Forward Inside Mohawk 4. Right Forward Inside Mohawk 5. Hockey Stop 6. Introduction to Backward Edges     Intermediate 2 (Delta equivalent) & Adult Intermediate 1. Left Forward Inside 3-Turn 2. Right Forward Inside 3-Turn 3. Forward Outside Edges 4. Forward Inside Edges 5. Lunge or Shoot the Duck 6. One-half Flip

PLEASE DO NOT ENROLL IN A CLASS NOT APPROPRIATE TO YOUR LEVEL!The adult curriculum is designed for beginning, intermediate and experienced adult skaters who wish to improve their skating skills. Participation in the program will help promote physical fitness and improve balance and coordination while teaching proper skating techniques. Group instruction includes a 30 minute class and 30 minute supervised practice. Intermediate 3 (Freestyle 1 equivalent) & Adult Intro to Advanced 1. Backward outside edges 2. Backward inside edges 3. Two-foot spin 4. Forward spiral 5. Forward inside pivot 6. Waltz jump   Tuesday Adult Advanced Taught by experienced coaches to cater to the desire of the class, The Tuesday Adult Advanced class goes beyong the fundamental skills of adult beginner and intermediate to learn advanced turns, changes of edge, spins, jumps, and sometimes choreographed step sequences.

All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor. 

Participants must check-in to the front desk prior to entering the gym.

Oakland Bears In-House Hockey Summer 2026! 

Bears In-House Hockey Summer 2026
Oakland Ice is excited to announce the registration is now open for the 2026 Summer Season!

Summer season will begin week of Monday July 13th, 2026. 

Summer season will end on Saturday August 29th, 2026.

Hockey Tot First introductory hockey/skating level for ages 3-6 Class Curriculum: Proper way to fall and get-up Marching while moving Two-foot jump in place Single Swizzle There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Note:
Class Time: 10:15am - 10:45am
Practice/Free time: 10:45 - 11:15am

Class curriculum:
Tiny Tot 1:

  1. Proper way to fall and get-up.
  2. Marching while moving
  3. Two-foot jump in place


There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Note:
Class Time: 10:15am - 10:45am
Practice/Free time: 10:45 - 11:15am

Tiny Tot 2:

  1. Beginner Two-Foot Glide
  2. Push and Glide Stroking
  3. Preparation for Snowplow Stop
  4. Dip
  5. Forward Swizzle

There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Note:
Class Time: 10:15am - 10:45am
Practice/Free time: 10:45 - 11:15am

Tiny Tot 3:

  1. T-Position and Push off Right & Left
  2. Backward Wiggles
  3. Two or One Foot Snowplow Stop
  4. Backward Swizzle



There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class Time: 10:15am - 10:45am Practice/Free time: 10:45 - 11:15am Beginner 1/Pre-Alpha First beginner level skating class for both Skating & Hockey ages 6-17 Class curriculum: Two-foot glide. One-foot glide/ right foot/ left foot. Forward Swizzle. Backward Wiggle. Backward Swizzle. Forward Pumps.There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Introducing show/performance and synchronized skating for our youth learn to skate participants. Synchronized skating is a fun way for all skaters at any level to improve edge quality, power, speed, and flexibility. Teaching basic synchronized skating elements, Line, Circle, Block, Wheel, and Intersection while skating in unison. Speed and extreme difficulty are not expected. Prerequisite: forward snowplow stop. 

Performances: TBD

All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor.

Class Curriculum:

  2. Backward inside edges
  3. Two-foot spin
  4. Forward spiral
  5. Forward inside pivot
  6. Waltz jump

There is No refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class time: 12:15pm- 12:45pm

Practice time: Public session 1:00pm-3:00pm

Advanced 1/ Freestyle 2 First Advanced level skating class for ages 6-17 Class Curriculum: Forward outside spirals Forward inside spirals One-half Lutz One-half Toe Walley One foot spin Ballet jump There is No refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class time: 12:15pm-12:45pm
Practice time: Public Session 1:00pm-3:00pm
Advanced 2/ Freestyle 3 Second Advanced level skating class for ages 6-17 Prerequisite: Must have completed Advanced 1 (Freestyle 2). Class Curriculum: Backward outside pivot Change foot spin. Backward spiral Salchow jump. Toe Loop jump Backward outside three turns There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class time: 12:15pm- 12:45pm
Practice time: Public session 1:00pm-3:00pm
Advanced 3/ Freestyle 4 Third Advanced level skating class for ages 6-17 Prerequisite: Must have completed Advanced 2 (Freestyle 3). Flip jump Loop jump Sit spin One-half loop jump Backward inside three-turns Backward inside spirals Backward outside spirals There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class time: 12:15pm-12:45pm 
Practice time: Public session 1:00pm-3:00pm

Advanced 4 (Freestyle 5-7) Maneuvers from all three of these levels will be introduced in this class. Skaters at this level are often split between mastering an axel or not. Skaters will be placed in appropriate group by evaluating speed, strength, and execution of maneuvers.

Pre-requisite: Must have completed Advanced 3 (Freestyle 4) at minimum.
There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Private Hockey Lessons

Players must be on the ice with a Sharks Ice private coach!

All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor. 

Participants must check-in to the front desk prior to entering the gym.

OIC - Gretzky Hour

Full Hockey Gear Required
Each player is required to check in at the front desk before each session and must wear their sticker receipt visible on their pants.
Full USA Hockey Certified equipment is required.
Limit 40 skaters and 4 goalies.
No outside or private coaches are allowed on the ice at any time. - Unless approved through management.
